channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Bungie Planning For Major Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Bungie is reportedly preparing major layoffs after Destiny 2’s final update as the studio shifts focus toward Marathon.

Reportedly, the developer, which is now a Sony subsidiary, doesn’t have a project lined up for the Destiny 2 team after the final update for that game comes on June 9. It is also said that the studio is investing more in Marathon, the extraction shooter title released in March. Since its release, the game has not met sales expectations, but the studio hopes the game will expand its player base over time. In recent months, some of the people working on Destiny were moved to work on Marathon.

Bungie Layoffs Reportedly Forthcoming as Studio Focuses on Marathon

According to the report from Jason Schreier of Bloomberg, Bungie is planning for a significant number of layoffs after Destiny 2’s final update. The studio does not have plans to enter production on a third Destiny title immediately. Regardless of the status of a Destiny 3 release, cuts appear to be on the horizon. It was not made clear how many people would be affected by the cuts.

Staff is looking to pitch new projects, including potential Destiny-related ones; however, nothing has been greenlit yet. The report also states that there’s no guarantee any project will move forward in the current challenging market, which has led Sony to raise prices on its console and subscription services.

Layoffs Are A Major Theme of 2026 in Gaming

Unfortunately, major layoffs have been a recurring theme throughout 2026 in the gaming landscape. Not only have major publishers like Ubisoft seen massive cuts and project cancellations. Many games have met an early demise. One such title was Highguard, which went from being the final game announced at the Game Awards to being active for less than 2 months. Learn more about those cuts here.

Learn more about the end of Destiny 2’s updates here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

How do you feel about this unfortunate news for Bungie? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Take-Two Plans to Release 29 Titles Through FY2029
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two Interactive plans to release 29 new titles through FY2029, including three new core franchise debuts and upcoming sequels, remakes, and platform extensions of current properties.

On May 21, Take-Two Interactive announced its earnings for the fiscal year ending in 2026. As part of their report, they revealed plans to release 29 games between FY2027 and FY2029. Among those titles are three mobile games, only CSR 3 from Zynga was named in that category, and eight sports titles. Three of those sports games are the 2027 iterations of NBA 2K, PGA Tour 2K, and WWE 2K. Additionally, they’re planning 15 titles within existing core IPs. These include, of course, the much-anticipated Grand Theft Auto 6, releasing on November 19.

More About Take-Two’s Ambitious Plans

The breakdown of those 15 titles from core existing properties includes eight sequels and seven games that are either remakes, remasters, or platform extensions. Perhaps most interestingly, they are planning on releasing three new core IP titles. Two of those three have been named. Judas, an upcoming game from Ghost Story Games, and Project ETHOS from 31st Union. There are several asterisks indicating that the existing roadmap may change.

“These titles are a snapshot of our current development pipeline. It is likely that some of these titles will not be developed through completion, that launch timing may change, and that we will also be adding new titles to our slate,” they state in the report.

For more from the recent earnings report, check out how much money Grand Theft Auto 6 is expected to generate for the company in the next fiscal year.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Grand Theft Auto 5 Keeps Selling Millions Every Quarter
item.content:encoded:
The sky is blue, water is wet, and Rockstar Games has sold even more copies of GTA 5.

In its latest quarterly earnings release, Take-Two Interactive revealed that Grand Theft Auto 5 has sold nearly 230 million copies. This is an increase from its last report, which noted the game had sold up to 225 million copies. This means the game is selling approximately 5 million games per quarter.

Grand Theft Auto 5 has been available since 2013 and has been released across three console generations and PC. Its successor, GTA 6, is slated for release on November 19, a date that has been reaffirmed twice recently following multiple delays.

More Notes Concerning GTA 5 and GTA Online

Additionally, the earnings report touted further details on the success of the GTA franchise, including that GTA 5 generated $1 billion in retail sales faster than any entertainment release in history. It was also touted that the franchise as a whole has sold over 470 million units, calling it one of the most successful and iconic brands in all of entertainment. Strauss Zelnick, Take-Two CEO, echoed these remarks in a recent interview. Check out his comments here

It’s expected that GTA 6 will generate $8 billion in revenue for the company in the next fiscal year. Learn more about those expectations here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor all things Grand Theft Auto ahead of November 19. We will report any updates as more information becomes available.

Are you excited for GTA 6? Are you surprised by the continued success of GTA 5? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced PS5 Pro Version Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced director has confirmed that a PS5 Pro version is definitely on the cards.

The upcoming title has been remade from the ground up, introducing enhanced visuals, additional content, new mechanics, and much more. However, players have been wondering how much more they can get out of the title visually, and it seems the title is being optimized for PS5 Pro’s latest upscaling tech, PSSR 2.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Devs ‘Definitely’ Want to Support PS5 Pro’s Upgraded PSSR

In a recent interview with Wccftech, Richard Knight, the director of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, confirmed that a PS5 Pro version of the game will be supported. Knight mentioned that he personally owns a PS5 Pro and that the development team wishes to implement features such as the upgraded PSSR. Additionally, players can expect enhanced performance and visual fidelity, similar to those in other games on the platform. However, no exact details were revealed.

For players looking for the best visual experience, consoles need look no further than the PS5 Pro. Sony has also provided players with an upgraded version of their upscaling tech, PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR). Several games already support the new tech, including Crimson Desert, Alan Wake, Resident Evil Requiem, 007 First Light, Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth, ARC Raiders, and more.

In other news, Ubisoft has revealed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, the upcoming remake, has already surpassed 1 million wishlists. Additionally, check out the minimum, recommended, high, and ultra PC system requirements for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. What are your thoughts on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced supporting PS5 Pro? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Take-Two reconfirming that the GTA 6 release date hasn’t changed. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: LEC Spring Split Playoffs 2026 Preview
item.content:encoded:
The LEC Spring Split 2026 Playoffs kick off this weekend (May 23) to crown EMEA’s new League of Legends champions.

As well as the trophy, teams will be competing for Mid-Season Invitational (MSI) and Esports World Cup qualification.

Though G2 Esports and Karmine Corp have already qualified for EWC. Should they win the Spring Split, Movistar KOI will join them at the $2 million international tournament.

The top six teams will compete in a partially double-elimination (not for NAVI or GIANTX) bracket made up entirely of best-of-fives.

G2 Esports vs Karmine Corp

Kicking off a LEC Spring Playoffs is a behemoth clash between the league’s two strongest teams on paper.

In a rematch of both the LEC Versus final and the recent Esports World Cup EMEA Qualifier final, G2 will be heavily favoured.

The LEC Versus final was a spectacular series, which went to all five games and was extremely close.

Meanwhile, the online meeting between the two last week resulted in a straight 3-0 stomp for G2, who appear to have levelled up substantially.

The key match-up in this series will likely be between the team’s two star junglers, G2’s Rudy ‘SkewMond’ Semaan and KCorp’s Martin ‘Yike’ Sundelin.

Skewmond has gotten the better of Yike in recent meetings, leaving a tough task for the KCorp pro if he wants to take down his former team.

Movistar KOI vs Team Vitality

The other Upper Bracket bout looks like a very interesting series between two inconsistent teams with very high ceilings.

Movistar KOI barely squeaked into the top four and have repeatedly lost to KCorp, yet they also defeated G2 in their last LEC series.

Meanwhile, Team Vitality has been the surprise team of the year, finishing first in the Spring regular season.

Yet, they were eliminated early from the EWC qualifiers after losing 2-1 to MKOI and a surprising 2-0 loss to Shifters

Both teams have had standout solo-laners who will likely be the difference makers in this next meeting.

Vitality toplaner Kaan ‘Naak Nako’ Okan has been a monster this, regularly accruing solo leads and making the difference in his team’s inspired form.

Similarly, Joseph Joon ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun has had a phenomenal split and regularly performed while his teammates around him have struggled.

The Lower Bracket Teams

Natus Vincere and GIANTX don’t get any second chances, as they will have to try and struggle through the Lower Bracket.

Neither team is considered to be on the same level as the top four, yet they have shown bright sparks.

NAVI have impressed throughout the year with explosive talent and appear to have gotten over their issues with closing out games.

Meanwhile, GIANTX don’t appear much better than last year, yet they recently defeated KCorp — though that may have had something to do with the opposing Brand top.

Still, these two teams have more time to practice and prepare, which could allow them to pull off a big upset or two.

Share your predictions for the LEC Spring Playoffs with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see the new EMEA Masters Spring format as Riot Games responded to community backlash.

MOBILE APP
